title: Infrastructure, Risk & Resilience
overview: Module 5 brings the full assessment lifecycle together. You'll deploy a simulated client environment, discover and score vulnerabilities, build a risk matrix, and compile everything into a professional Engagement Packet — the same deliverable a real security consultant hands to a client.

weeks_breakdown.labDesc: Deploy LoneStar DevOps infrastructure, conduct vulnerability scanning, score top 10 findings with CVSS 3.1, build risk matrix and remediation roadmap.
Compile findings into a professional Engagement Packet. Executive summary for Amanda Nguyen (investors), technical runbook and remediation roadmap for Sarah Martinez.
No lab assignment this week. Review Chapters 10 and 20 and your Lab 5.1 work in preparation for the Module 5 Exam.

clientContext.label: Client Context: LoneStar DevOps (Austin, TX)
clientContext.body: LoneStar is an 85-person SaaS startup with $12M ARR and a Series B on the horizon. Their infrastructure was set up by a developer who left six months ago. An enterprise prospect requires a third-party security assessment before signing. Amanda Nguyen (CEO) needs a report she can hand to Longhorn Ventures investors. Sarah Martinez (Lead DevOps) needs a roadmap she can actually execute with two engineers and no staging environment.

footer: Start with Lab 5.1 in the Assignments section.

notice.label: 🎯 Capstone Module:
notice.body: This is the complete engagement workflow in two weeks: discover, score, prioritize, and report. You'll draw on skills from every previous module — reconnaissance methodology, network scanning, cryptographic concepts, and attack awareness — and apply them to a real risk management deliverable.
